name: Marta the Baker
description: A plump, flour-dusted woman with rosy cheeks and a warm smile. The scent of fresh bread follows her everywhere.

start.text: Welcome, welcome! Fresh bread just out of the oven. Can I tempt you?
start.narrative: Marta wipes her hands on her apron and beams at you.

responses.text: Smells wonderful. What do you have?

wares.text: Sourdough loaves, honey cakes, and my famous cinnamon rolls. The fisherman trades me fresh catch for bread — I make a lovely fish pie too, if I do say so myself.
wares.narrative: She gestures to the counter laden with baked goods.

gossip.text: Well now... Aldric the jeweler's been buying strange gems from that peddler at the crossroads. And I hear the acolyte in the catacombs has been acting odd — chanting at all hours. Oh, and Gorath finally forged that blade he's been working on for months.
gossip.narrative: She leans across the counter, lowering her voice with relish.

gems.text: Glowing things, they were. Not natural, if you ask me. The peddler claims they came from the forest, but I reckon they're from the ruins. Aldric won't say what he's doing with them.
gems.narrative: She purses her lips and shakes her head.

acolyte.text: Hooded figure, lurks down in the ossuary. Used to be quiet and pious, but lately? Muttering about seals and bindings. The priestess is worried — she told me herself over tea.

responses.text: Maybe I should visit the temple.
